How to Acquire
Requires purchasing the Elegant Key from Sly in Dirtmouth after obtaining the Shopkeeper's Key.

The Spell is located behind a locked door in Soul Sanctum within the City of Tears. Requires defeating a Soul Warrior that continuously spawns Folly.

Trivia

There is an unused version of Shade Soul that consists of a mixture between a black Shade Soul and a white Vengeful Spirit much like how Abyss Shriek combines black souls with white Howling Wraiths.

Shade Soul is obtained from a Snail Shamans linked to some kind of machine.

ZH:暗影之魂

---

---

# 文档：Silkspear

- 类别：abilities
- 标识：silkspear
- 来源：wiki/Silkspear

Silkspear is a Tools Hornet unlocks in Act 1 of Hollow Knight: Silksong. A strong offensive attack that attacks from a distance.

Function
A powerful forward stab that deals significant damage, and it is also a key ability for opening up some map areas; portions of the map and the Bell Beast are trapped behind regenerating walls of silk that standard attacks cannot break, but a Silkspear strike can clear them.

---

---

# 文档：World Sense

- 类别：abilities
- 标识：world-sense
- 来源：wiki/World_Sense

<i>Vessel. Though bound, you shall know the state of the world.</i>

Function
Displays current Completion (Hollow Knight) percentage in the inventory and on the save file in the main menu. Also shows the number of enemies encountered and required in the Hunter's Journal.

How to Acquire
Found in the Temple of the Black Egg once it has been opened by killing all three Dreamers. The ability itself is granted by inspecting the Lore Tablets (Hollow Knight)#Forgotten Crossroads beside the Bench (Hollow Knight).

Trivia
The World Sense icon appears on the <i>Completion</i> Achievements (Hollow Knight).
The name of this ability is not mentioned in the game itself but only in the game files; <i>"world_sense_prompt"</i> is the name of the sprite seen when acquiring the ability.

---

---

# 文档：Brumm

- 类别：characters
- 标识：brumm
- 来源：wiki/Brumm

<b>Brumm</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He is involved in the Grimm Troupe (Quest) of The Grimm Troupe (Lore).

Lore
Brumm is a member of the Grimm Troupe. Although he initially encourages the Knight to search for flames for the Grimmchild, he begins having second thoughts about the Ritual later on.

He does not approve of the Ritual playing itself out repeatedly and endlessly, considering himself and the rest of the Troupe slaves to it. Brumm believes the gathering of flames desecrates the dead Kingdom of Hallownest. He wants to make the Ritual fail by destroying the Nightmare Lantern, the anchor keeping the Grimm Troupe in Hallownest.

In-game events
When the Grimm Troupe is first summoned, he can be found playing the accordion just inside of Grimm tent in Dirtmouth.

After Grimm has been defeated, Brumm appears in Distant Village where he gives the Knight a Flame if they do not already have three Flames. He then offers to help the Knight banish the Grimm Troupe. Should the Knight complete the Ritual instead, he explains that he bears them no hatred.

Brumm is next found in the Howling Cliffs in the room of the Nightmare Lantern, where he jabs his staff into the brazier so it can be broken.

If the Grimm Troupe is banished, Brumm disappears, and an amnesic bug named Nymm appears in Dirtmouth who greatly resembles him.

Location
Brumm appears in three locations depending on how far the Grimm Troupe's quest has progressed.

Achievements

Trivia
Brumm's accordion music in Dirtmouth can also be heard while standing outside the tent.
The accordion that Brumm plays has limbs, implying that it is a dead or live bug.
Brumm's name within the game's files is "IGOR" a possible reference to wikipedia:Igor (character) in the Universal Pictures adaptations of the novel <i>wikipedia:Frankenstein</i>.

---

---

# 文档：Cloth

- 类别：characters
- 标识：cloth
- 来源：wiki/Cloth

<b>Cloth</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Wanderers in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She is on a quest of her own throughout Hallownest to prove her bravery.

Lore
Cloth is a large cicada warrior. She set out to Hallownest in order to prove her courage by defeating mighty creatures. However, her cowardly nature often takes over her bravado and leads to hiding underground to escape her foes or to simply sleep, snoring loudly while doing so. She is motivated by the prospect of dying in an epic battle and joining her departed Nola.

Cloth fights by brandishing a club cut from the tooth of an ancient beast.

Later on, if Cloth has yet to be encountered in the Ancient Basin, she can be found at the beginning of the Failed Tramway in Deepnest. There she rests, exhausted and sore after her fight against the ferocious creatures of the Nest. She plans to leave the area later and search for a less terrifying challenge.

In the Ancient Basin, Cloth found herself unable to fend off the creatures infesting the end of the Tram tunnel and buried herself to hide from them. Once the Knight clears off the tunnel of Aspid Hunters and Lesser Mawleks, she comes out from the ground. Abandoning her bravado, she openly confides her weaknesses.

From this point on, Cloth can be encountered at the entrance of the Queen's Gardens near the Fog Canyon. She returned to this area to face Mantis Tribe#The Mantis Traitors occupying the gardens. She hopes to finally be able to face down her fears this time or die doing so. She later appears to help the Knight during an ambush of Mantis Traitors near the White Lady refuge, and they fight together against the Traitor Lord. However, the Traitor Lord mortally wounds her at the end of the fight, she then chooses to deal the final blow herself before dying.

Once the Knight leaves the area and returns, Spirits#Cloth can be found above her corpse. She is thrilled by her fight, having achieved what she came to Hallownest for, and states she will depart soon to join Nola. In her mind, she still hopes to be reunited with Nola in death.
<i>"Fun Fact: Cloth was originally called Burlap until we realised burlap, the material, doesn't exist in Hallownest so he underwent a name change. We’re still working with Noah to finalise Cloth so look forward to his exciting (possibly tragic) tale when you play the final game."</i>
Although pitched as male during the Kickstarter campaign, the official manual denotes Cloth as female.
There is always rumbling before Cloth pops out of the ground in any area; this is accompanied by the same sound effects as when the Knight walks on a breakable floor.
Her club can be seen resting on the grave of the Traitors' Child in the background of the Traitor Lord fight in Godhome, if she is killed in battle.
If the Dream Nail has not been obtained before defeating the Traitor Lord with Cloth, her spirit is invisible, but can still be interacted with to hear her dialogue.
Although Nola is mentioned several times by Cloth, Nola cannot be seen in the game. The circumstances of their death are also unknown.

---

---

# 文档：Distant Villagers

- 类别：characters
- 标识：distant-villagers
- 来源：wiki/Distant_Villagers

The <b>Distant Villagers</b> are NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. They attempt to get the Knight to sit on the first Bench (Hollow Knight) in Beast's Den.

In-game events
The Distant Villagers are found in the entrance room to the Beast's Den in the Distant Village of Deepnest.

They can be listened to and all soothingly try to convince the Knight to sit on the Bench in the middle of the room. However, after the Knight sits on it, they are then stuck there by webs, and all the Distant Villagers converge upon the Bench, causing the music to distort and the screen to fade to black. After a series of violent sound effects and a loud roar, the Knight is then transported into Beast's Den.

After the Knight has been transported into Beast's Den or when Herrah has been defeated, the Distant Villagers abandon their cloaks and shells and are never seen again.

Trivia
They cannot be Dream Nailed.
In the other cocoon of Distant Village there is a wrapped-up mound of husks that, when Dream Nailed, gives a warning and foreshadow the Distant Villagers:
<i>"...They lied..."</i>
<i>"...Not friends..."</i>

---

---

# 文档：Divine

- 类别：characters
- 标识：divine
- 来源：wiki/Divine

<b>Divine</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. Her primary role is allowing the Knight to upgrade Fragile Charms to their  Unbreakable counterparts.

Lore
Divine is a member of The Grimm Troupe (Lore). She is looking for the source of the smell deep below her, Leg Eater. She eats the Fragile Charms that he created when the Knight gives them to her, only giving them back as Unbreakable Charms after being given a large amount of Geo.

file:Leg_Eater_Death.png
If the Knight goes to Leg Eater after upgrading all of the Fragile Charms to their Unbreakable variants while also having one of them equipped, he says that he "smells" her and goes above ground to find her.

When the Knight returns to Divine, Leg Eater's dismembered claws can be seen on the floor in front of her. After this, Divine leaves with the rest of the Grimm Troupe.

In-game events
She arrives in Dirtmouth along with the rest of the Troupe with the lighting of the Nightmare Lantern. Should the Knight choose the Banishment ending and destroy the Nightmare Lantern, Divine disappears with the Troupe, leaving any currently-eaten Charm where her tent was. However, she only disappears upon the defeat of Nightmare King Grimm if the Knight has already upgraded all three Fragile Charms and has listened to Leg Eater.

Unbreakable Charms
Divine acts as a late-game Geo sink. Compared to other Merchants, her offers are the most expensive. All of her exchanges put together total . While the cost of her services is extreme, so is the payoff. The Fragile Charms carry some of the most powerful passive effects in the game, and removing their "on death" stipulation allows them to be used much more freely.
For , she upgrades Fragile Greed to Unbreakable Greed
For , she upgrades Fragile Heart to Unbreakable Heart
For , she upgrades Fragile Strength to Unbreakable Strength

In order to perform an exchange, the Knight must have one of the Fragile Charms equipped. After listening to Divine, she requests the Charm be given to her. If given, she eats the Charm. By continually listening to her, Divine eventually requests Geo. If paid the requested amount, she excretes the Unbreakable variant of the previously ingested Charm. If the Knight has more than one Fragile Charm equipped the Divine will eat the Charms in the order they appear on the Charm Page, i.e. Health, Geo, Strength. To allow her to eat another Charm the Knight must pay for the Unbreakable version of the previous charm.

Location
Divine is located in Dirtmouth after the Grimm Troupe has been summoned. She resides in a smaller tent west of the main one.

Trivia
Due to how the Banishment ending works, the Unbreakable Charms are permanently unobtainable if the Knight decides to banish the Troupe.
If Divine has a Charm currently consumed when the Grimm Troupe is banished, then it is left on the ground, still in its Fragile form, when returning to Dirtmouth.
When Divine eats a Charm, the Knight is considered to have one less Charm for the purposes of Salubra's shop and Completion (Hollow Knight) percentage.

References

---

---

# 文档：Elderbug

- 类别：characters
- 标识：elderbug
- 来源：wiki/Elderbug

<b>Elderbug</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He is the oldest resident of Dirtmouth and a friendly face for new travellers seeking the Hallownest.

Lore
While Elderbug is the oldest bug in Dirtmouth, he was not around before the fall of Hallownest, the Stag Station of Dirtmouth being closed even before his time.

Since many or all of Dirtmouth's residents have disappeared down the well into the Forgotten Crossroads, Elderbug is the only remaining resident in town and greets the Knight at the beginning of the game. These bugs followed their dreams and then never returned, making him believe that dreams are not so great after all. He has become pessimistic, seeing the world as faded, with dark thoughts creeping into his mind.

Elderbug had an old friend who is buried in the graveyard of Dirtmouth. He visits her grave occasionally.

In-game events
When Elderbug is first met, he warns the Knight to be careful if they decide to head down the well, as creatures who go there turn mad and travellers are robbed of their memories.

Throughout the Knight's journey, Elderbug gives hints on which Areas (Hollow Knight) they should go to next. He also comments on other residents returning to Dirtmouth and buildings reopening.

When he is given a Delicate Flower, he happily accepts it and notes that he had not expected any gratitude for the advice that he gave to travellers. The flower makes him see the world as a little less faded and causes him to consider that dreams might not be so bad after all.

After The Grimm Troupe (Lore) arrives in Dirtmouth, Elderbug moves from his usual spot near the Bench (Hollow Knight) to a spot west of this. He comments on how the Grimm Troupe's arrival fills him with dread, and that he would best ignore it. He moves back to the Bench after this.

Once the Grimm Troupe leaves or is banished, Elderbug expresses how relieved he is that the town is quiet once more. If the Troupe was banished, he finds Nymm outside of town and invites him in. The musician's arrival delights him, as Nymm's accordion music gently breaks Dirtmouth's windy sounds.

Location
Elderbug is located in Dirtmouth, next to the Bench.

Trivia
Although he is a valid recipient of the Delicate Flower, giving him the Flower does not have any effect and only counts as it being destroyed. Another Flower can still be acquired from the Grey Mourner to be delivered to the grave in the Queen's Gardens, or from the grave itself if the Grey Mourner's Delicate Flower (Quest) has already been completed.
If Elderbug is ignored when first entering Dirtmouth, he calls after the Knight in surprise and, if listened to later, comments on this.
Elderbug was used as an example of a Cut Content (Hollow Knight) of the Dream Nail known as "Dream Diving". With this ability it was possible to enter the "Dream Well" of characters, which contained their hopes, fears and secrets. was released showing the Knight use the Dream Nail on Elderbug, which popped the top of his head off and opened a portal that the Knight could enter. Inside Elderbug's Dream Well was a young version of him that could be listened to. This ability was later cut from the game, but Elderbug's separate head sprite could still be found in the game's files prior to the  <i>:Category: Lifeblood</i> update.

---

---

# 文档：Emilitia

- 类别：characters
- 标识：emilitia
- 来源：wiki/Emilitia

<b>Eternal Emilitia</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. Her room provides access to a shortcut between the City of Tears and the east side of the Royal Waterways.

Lore
Emilitia used to be renowned amongst the upper caste of Hallownest but was at some point cast out by her fellows. While the rest of Hallownest's higher class has died, their bodies walking around mindlessly in the City of Tears, Emilitia managed to survive. This fact brings her great happiness, causing her to laugh to herself constantly in her room.

In-game events
Emilitia can be found in a hidden room in the City of Tears. Her room is first only accessible through a series of hidden walls in the Royal Waterways, but after the door is opened from within it can be re-entered at any point, providing an alternative entrance to the Waterways.

She can be offered a Delicate Flower, but does not accept it as she believes the Flower's pure aura is no match for the light of the Pale King.

Location

References

---

---

# 文档：Fluke Hermit

- 类别：characters
- 标识：fluke-hermit
- 来源：wiki/Fluke_Hermit

<b>Fluke Hermit</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She provides lore on the Flukes.

Lore
Fluke Hermit is an uninfected Fluke. She gathers some of the junk that collects in and around the Junk Pit, regarding it as treasure. She notes that her mother's "glow" appears angry, hinting at Flukemarm being Infection. After Flukemarm's death, she wonders if she is now the new mother.

In-game events
Fluke Hermit can be found within a secret room in the Junk Pit, prodding a small fluke. After completing two Pantheons, she leaves her room and can be found peering down at the Junk Pit, wishing that the Godseeker would go away. She returns to her room afterwards. Fluke Hermit has new dialogue when she is visited while having the Flukenest Charms equipped.

Location
Fluke Hermit is located through a crack in the wall along the northeast corner of the Junk Pit that is accessed by going up and entering a hidden passage on the water.

Trivia
Fluke Hermit is the only fluke met that appears to have sentience and is able to speak. It is unknown whether all flukes were like her before they became Infected.

References

---

---

# 文档：Godseeker

- 类别：characters
- 标识：godseeker
- 来源：wiki/Godseeker

The <b>Godseeker</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Quest NPCs in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i> who travelled to Hallownest seeking "gods", powerful beings revered for their strength and power. In her mind, she exists in a different shape and carries her tribe of Godseekers, with whom she shares a collective mind. They inhabit a Dream Realm of their own making: Godhome. She starts the quest of <i>Godmaster</i>.

Lore

The Godseekers originate from a land called the Land of Storms. Higher Beings#Gods of Thunder, Gods of Rain either died or abandoned them, At some point, they merged within the mind of a single Godseeker. They also built a temporary tuning device in order to find the resonance of other powerful beings in distant lands.

The current Godseeker bearing the mask ended up in Hallownest following the remnants of the Pale King power. With the King's vanishing, she hopes to attune instead Godhome to the "God of Gods" of Hallownest, and commune with them - initially assuming this "God of Gods" to be the Radiance. For this, she uses the Godtuner to attune the mind or essence of Hallownest's most powerful creatures to Godhome, where they can become gods.

There, the Godseekers observe their fights within the Pantheons. With each fight, they aim to ascend to and commune with even higher, out-of-reach creatures, until reaching the Radiance herself. She ended up in a chained, sarcophagus-like cocoon, which was washed away down the Royal Waterways.

In-game events
The Godseeker's cocoon can be found deep in the Junk Pit and can be unlocked with a Simple Key. When the Godseeker is freed, she automatically drops her Godtuner. The Knight can pick it up and start seeking gods for her.

In Hallownest, the Godseeker gradually wakes up with each pantheon the Knight completes. She encourages them to seek gods for her, rewarding them only with her sight and presence. In Godhome however, the Godseeker berates the Knight for their intrusion. She later gains interest in them on their way to ascend past the Pure Vessel, noting both vessels' similarities. The Godseeker finally opens up to the Knight and encourages them as they ascend throughout the final Pantheon. She even shares with them her despair of not being able to tune to Hallownest's other Higher Beings.

When the Knight finally ascends past the Absolute Radiance, they become Void Entity#Void Given Focus and become the new focus of the godseekers' communion. However, this new God of Gods overtakes both Godhome, the godseekers' tribe and the Godseeker's physical body. If the Delicate Flower was given to the Godseeker before the Knight's final ascension, the flower makes the Void Given Focus vanish instead of being freed into Hallownest.

Location
Godseeker is found both in the Junk Pit and Godhome.

Achievements

Trivia
Team Cherry have stated that they cannot say who locked Godseeker in her cocoon, but noted that it is not clear whether she built the cocoon herself or somebody else did.
In Godhome, whenever an arena has been completed, the Godseeker sitting on her throne faces the sky before the arena transition animation comes in. During the arena fight, the Godseeker usually looks at the Knight. However, she looks at the boss instead in the following situations:
Hornet Protector and Hornet Sentinel intro animation.
Dung Defender outro animation.
Soul Master rapid teleporting when he transitions to his second Phase and his outro.
Winged Nosk intro animation.
Brothers Oro & Mato second Phase intro animation, when Mato joins.
Great Nailsage Sly entire second Phase.
Troupe Master Grimm intro animation or his entire fight if he has been hit while he was bowing.
Nightmare King Grimm intro animation.
Pure Vessel intro and outro animations.
Absolute Radiance intro animation.
The Godseeker in the Junk Pit resembles an early concept art piece created by Ari Gibson.
When found in the Tuner Memory, the Godseeker is stuck in the cave wall for unknown reasons. She also appears to be tiny, as the length between her fore-claws and back-claws is much less than it is in the present. However, it is unknown what her body looks like as it is hidden in shadow with only the claws and head visible.

---

---

# 文档：Grey Mourner

- 类别：characters
- 标识：grey-mourner
- 来源：wiki/Grey_Mourner

<b>Grey Mourner</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Quest NPCs in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She is involved in the Delicate Flower (Quest), where she asks the Knight to deliver a Delicate Flower to her lover's grave in the Queen's Gardens.

Lore

Grey Mourner's real name is Ze'mer. She used to be one of the Five Great Knights of Hallownest. She is not originally from Hallownest, being native to an area outside of the kingdom known as "lands serene". It is from this place that she also brought Delicate Flowers. Some time after her Mantis lover died, Grey Mourner retreated to her house in the Resting Grounds where she turned to mourning.

In-game events
Grey Mourner is found in the east area of the Resting Grounds, behind breakable walls. Reaching her requires Desolate Dive or Descending Dark.

She talks about her dead lover and requests that the Knight takes a Delicate Flower to her grave, located in the Queen's Gardens. This Flower breaks if the Knight uses the Stagways, teleports with the Dreamgate, or takes damage.

(Note: Using Tram Stations to travel is safe, and does not break the Flower)

She dissipates after successfully completing this quest, leaving behind a Mask Shard.

Location
Grey Mourner is found residing in a house in a hidden area in the east of the Resting Grounds. The ceiling above a chest needs to be broken to access her location. Her residence is referred to as the "Grey Mansion" on the Areas (Hollow Knight).

Achievements

Trivia
The full name of the Hollow Knight Kickstarter who created Grey Mourner is <b>Xun Zhao</b>.
Grey Mourner has one line of Cut Content (Hollow Knight) which would have been triggered by obtaining the King's Brand. However, this line is not used in the game nor in Grey Mourner's dialogue programming:
<i>Le'mer, takes that heavy mantle...Che' wouldn't wish it upon one. Having served that brand at its great height, che' knows well what suffering it brings. Enough to turn nym'King to flee or fair Knights to mourning.</i>

---

---

# 文档：Grimmsteed

- 类别：characters
- 标识：grimmsteed
- 来源：wiki/Grimmsteed

The <b>Grimmsteeds</b> are two NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. They appear together with The Grimm Troupe (Lore) in Dirtmouth.

Lore
The Grimmsteeds are two faithful bugs who are part of the Grimm Troupe. They are possibly the ones who pull the Troupe's caravan. these bugs are probably based on a giraffe weevil.

References

---

---

# 文档：Herrah

- 类别：characters
- 标识：herrah
- 来源：wiki/Herrah

<b>Herrah the Beast</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She is one of the Dreamers and is involved in the Knight quest to destroy the Infection.

Lore
Herrah was the Weavers queen of the Spider Tribe of Deepnest and is the mother of Hornet. Her partner was part of the honoured caste, while Herrah herself is only a common beast. At a certain point, her partner died, leaving Herrah without an heir.  When the Infection first appeared, the Pale King asked Herrah to become a Dreamer to seal the entrance to the Black Egg in the Temple of the Black Egg. She only agreed if the Pale King would have a child with her, which ended up being Hornet. After Hornet was born, Herrah and Hornet were permitted little time together as Herrah went into an eternal sleep. She and the other Dreamers attempt to lock the Knight in the Dream Realm after the Resting Grounds encounter.

Once the Knight reaches her physical body in Beast's Den, her mind can be entered by using the Dream Nail. She can then be attacked in her Dream_Realm#Dreamers_Stage_1 after which Focus can be used to break her Seal. Her physical body has disappeared when the Knight wakes up. Hornet appears at Herrah's former resting place if she was fought in Kingdom's Edge before Herrah's death.

Location
Herrah's physical body is located in Beast's Den in Deepnest.

Achievements

Trivia
Early in development, Herrah was called Hegemol. This name was reused for one of the Five Great Knights.

Herrah has several Cut Content (Hollow Knight) of dialogue which can still be found in the game's files.
When trying to attack Herrah's physical body in Beast's Den, a Seals appears over her body.

---

---

# 文档：Iselda

- 类别：characters
- 标识：iselda
- 来源：wiki/Iselda

<b>Iselda</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She sells maps and mapping tools at her shop in Dirtmouth.

Lore
Iselda used to be a fighter; however, when she followed her husband Cornifer, his thirst for exploration leading them to Hallownest, she put down her weapon to enjoy a quieter life in Dirtmouth. Despite her tall stature, the two took residence in a small house next to the town's Stag Station from which she operates their shop.

She wishes Cornifer would spend more time at home and worries about his health and safety. She has always declined to join her husband on his adventures beyond the Forgotten Crossroads. She doesn't find much interest in shopkeeping, yet takes great care in crafting cartography pins while taking into account her customers' needs.

In-game events
Iselda opens up her store after the Knight listens to Cornifer, defeats the False Knight or skips him by leaving the battle. She sells Map and Quill and mapping tools.

These maps appear in Iselda's shop after Cornifer has left his spot in each respective area and has left his calling card. They share the same icon and are priced higher than what Cornifer initially offers for each.

scope="col" | Area
scope="col" | Description
scope="col" style="min-width: 5rem;"| Cost
Ancient Basin
A map of the Ancient Basin, a land buried deep down at the bottom of the world.

City of Tears
A map of the City of Tears, capital of Hallownest. Does water really fall from above the city? It sounds beautiful...

Crystal Peak
A map of Crystal Peak, the huge shining mountain that looms behind Dirtmouth. My husband brought back some crystals from the mines along with the map of this area. What makes them shine so brightly?

Deepnest
A map of Deepnest, a dark and dangerous area at the bottom of the world.

Fog Canyon
A map of Fog Canyon, which connects Greenpath to the Fungal Wastes. The air is thick there, so Cornifer found it difficult to find his way. Well, more difficult than he usually does.

Forgotten Crossroads
A map of the Forgotten Crossroads, the area just below Dirtmouth. Cornifer dragged me down there once. It was an empty, lonely place but still quite impressive.

Fungal Wastes
A map of Fungal Wastes, an area choked with fungus beneath the Forgotten Crossroads. My husband brought some mushrooms back from there and tried to cook them. The stench was hideous!

Greenpath
A map of Greenpath, a verdant area of Hallownest close to the surface. Apparently the water there is deadly to the touch!

Howling Cliffs
A map of the Howling Cliffs, which look over Dirtmouth. Cornifer told me that the winds got so loud there he couldn't hear himself speak. Which begs the question...Why was he speaking to himself?

Kingdom's Edge
A map of Kingdom's Edge, all the way out at the end of the world. My husband...He travels to such distant places, while I stay here in town.

Resting Grounds
A map of the Resting Grounds, a sacred place not far from the Crossroads. Cornifer told me he found the area serene and calming, but it sounds a bit spooky to me. No thanks.

Royal Waterways
A map of the Royal Waterways, the pipes and chambers below the City of Tears. It sounds like Cornifer really enjoyed exploring the area, though he was damp and shivering when he returned.

Queen's Gardens
A map of the Queen's Gardens, a royal sanctuary close to Greenpath. Cornifer sometimes calls me his Queen...Usually only when I'm angry though.

style="min-width: 5rem;"| Cost

Highly recommended! If you're having trouble finding your way in the maze of ruins below us, try this charm.

You'll need this if you want to update your map with new areas as you explore.

Iselda's shop is located in Dirtmouth, east of the Stag Station.

Even without buying the Cocoon Pin from Iselda, a glitched Cocoon Pin is already placed on the map for the Lifeblood Cocoon in the Failed Tramway.

# 文档：Jiji

- 类别：characters
- 标识：jiji
- 来源：wiki/Jiji

<b>Confessor Jiji</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She is a hidden resident of Dirtmouth who can summon unrecovered Shades.

Lore
Residents of Dirtmouth have long pondered on a mysterious stone door at the base of the Crystal Peak beyond their graveyard. Suggestions were once made to forcibly open the door, but awful groaning from within kept the townsfolk at bay.

She was asleep there for a very long time but was woken up when the Knight entered. She notes that the land is now covered with the stains of regret, and wonders what would happen to her should she fall asleep again.

Jiji makes mention of her "masters" whom she also shares with Steel Soul Jinn. These masters oppose order and have minds that are stated to be other. They are described as being different from Jinn and more complete.

In-game events

Confessor Jiji is found behind a locked door in Dirtmouth that requires a Simple Key to open.

In return for Rancid Eggs, Jiji offers a service to summon the Knight's Shade from wherever it spawned in the world. Upon summoning the Shade, it must still be defeated to restore the Knight's SOUL meter and lost Geo. This service is very useful if the Shade is in a difficult to reach area or has a lot of Geo on it.

Location
Confessor Jiji is located on the very east side of Dirtmouth, within a cave that requires a Simple Key to open.

Trivia
In Steel Soul Mode, Confessor Jiji is replaced by Steel Soul Jinn who offers Geo for Rancid Eggs.
Performing a ritual and summoning a Shade will blow out all the candles in Jiji's cave.
Having Jiji summon the Shade, killing it, and instantly leaving the room results in the SOUL meter remaining broken, though the Knight recovers their lost Geo and Jiji's dialogue is the same as when there is no lingering Shade.

---

---

# 文档：Jinn

- 类别：characters
- 标识：jinn
- 来源：wiki/Jinn

<b>Steel Soul Jinn</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She only appears in Steel Soul Mode, replacing Confessor Jiji.

Lore
Jinn resides in the locked cavern by the base of Crystal Peak in Dirtmouth, where she has been sleeping. She has unseen masters who do not seek order. Jinn speaks in rhythm and claims to be too young to tell anything about herself beside her purpose of providing and trading. Her metallic body cannot take damage, although she still reacts to strikes at her.

In-game events
Steel Soul Jinn replaces Jiji in Steel Soul Mode, as the difficulty mode's permanent death makes Jiji's service useless. She is disgusted by Rancid Eggs, but will still buy them for  each, as a favor to a friend which she claims appreciates them. This is also an excuse for her to get rid of the Geo she accumulated after helping or hurting other creatures, despite not wanting any.

Location
Steel Soul Jinn is located on the very east side of Dirtmouth, within a cave that requires a Simple Key to open.

Trivia

The friend she mentions who likes Rancid Eggs is most likely Confessor Jiji, who is willing to offer her services for them.
Jinn is one of the few NPCs who can be "hit" with the Nail without taking damage.
An unused Enemies (Silksong) revealed in the <i>Hollow Knight: Silksong</i> trailer bears a striking similarity to Jinn. It was supposed to be also a companion to Steel Assassin Sharpe.
Jinn is mistakenly referred to as male in some translations of the game.

References

---

---

# 文档：Joni

- 类别：characters
- 标识：joni
- 来源：wiki/Joni

<b>Blue Child Joni</b> is a Spirits in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. The Joni's Blessing Charms can be found on her corpse.

Lore
Joni was known as "the kindly heretic". She gave a blessing which is able to transfigure vital fluids into blue lifeblood.

Like all other spirits, the Dream Nail can be used on her, giving 1 Essence.

Location
Joni's spirit and corpse are located in Joni's Repose in the Howling Cliffs.

Trivia

Joni is named after Joni Kunelius, the Hollow Knight Kickstarter who designed her.
Joni was first revealed in a Hollow Knight Kickstarter detailing various Backer Bugs:
<i>Joni is a blue ground beetle and one of the teams' favourite bugs! Joni might be dead, but she's still quite happy. She wishes the player a safe journey through Hallownest.</i>

References

---

---

# 文档：Leg Eater

- 类别：characters
- 标识：leg-eater
- 来源：wiki/Leg_Eater

<b>Leg Eater</b> is a hermit NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He sells and repairs Charms for Geo.

Lore
Leg Eater is blind but has a powerful sense of smell. He is fixated on earning Geo, believing that if he has enough of it, it will make him become a king. Despite his own greed, he is prone to accusing others of the same sin, even wrongfully.

In-game events
Leg Eater is found in a camp in the Fungal Wastes, near the entrance to the Forgotten Crossroads. When first meeting him, he offers to show the Knight "something nice" for .

After being paid, Leg Eater opens his Charm shop and sells Fragile Charms that break upon death. He provides a repair service for broken Charms in exchange for a fee. If the Knight wears the Defender's Crest Charm, he appreciates the smell and sells and repairs his Charms at a 20% discount.

Leg Eater notices if the Knight wears one or two Unbreakable Charms bought from Divine of The Grimm Troupe (Lore). Enamoured by her pheromones, he seeks her after the Knight presents to him the full set of Unbreakable Charms.

After this, he disappears from his camp and his claws can be found resting in Divine's room. Divine comments that she liked this final "gift" from the Knight, suggesting Leg Eater's gruesome fate.

style="width: 96px;"| Item
Description
style="min-width: 9rem;"| Geo cost

/

/

/

/

<i>It's broken now, and its power is sleeping. Very sad! Give me Geo, and I'll fix it.</i>
/

Location
Leg Eater is located in the northern region of the Fungal Wastes. He can be easily found by the bodies of deceased bugs that appear outside his camp.

Trivia
The "campfire" beside Leg Eater is made out of a glass-like material and looks similar to a broken Fragile Charm.
It is possible that Leg Eater and Divine are the same species as the upper half of their bodies are almost identical.

---

# 文档：Lemm

- 类别：characters
- 标识：lemm
- 来源：wiki/Lemm

<b>Relic Seeker Lemm</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He collects Relics and buys them for fair amounts of Geo.

Lore
Lemm moved into his shop in the City of Tears at some point after the place was already empty and everyone in the tower was dead. He explores his current location and covets antiques, gleaning any information on the land's history.

While Lemm happily discusses his discoveries and hypotheses, he guards his collection and is not willing to sell any of it. He does not seek company unless they bring him more material for his collection.

In-game events
Lemm can be found at his shop overlooking the Fountain Square. He eagerly offers Geo in exchange for rare antiques from about the Kingdom: Wanderer's Journals, Hallownest Seals, King's Idols and Arcane Eggs. Unlike Geo, Relics are not lost upon death, which makes them a method of securing large amounts of Geo.

Upon delivery of a Relic, Lemm shares his collected knowledge of the kingdom's history: the Pale King and his Five Great Knights, the wanderers lost in the caverns, and even the Ancient Civilisation buried underneath.

After the Knight has defeated any one of the three Dreamers, Lemm closes his shop for a break. He can be found gazing at the Memorial to the Hollow Knight in the middle of the Fountain Square. Lemm ponders the meaning of the statue, as he never found the Hollow Knight mentioned anywhere else except for the fountain's plaque. After having listened to him, he returns to his shop.

style="min-width: 8rem;" | Item
Description
style="min-wdith: 5rem;" | Selling Price
amount
total worth

<i>These journals give us an interesting glimpse into the minds and hearts of those who lived before us.</i>
<i>They're a common find, so I can't offer you too much for them.</i>
---

4
4800

Location
Relic Seeker Lemm is located west of the statue of the Hollow Knight in the City of Tears. His shop initially has to be accessed from above.

Trivia

While Lemm mentions other Relic Seekers, no other such NPC is ever found in Hallownest.
While wearing the Defender's Crest Charms Lemm refuses to purchase any Relics.
According to screenshots from the Hollow Knight Press Kit, Lemm was originally going to be called <b>Relic Seeker Marm</b>.
The Nailsmith's voicelines were originally recorded for Lemm. Matthew 'Leth' Griffin, the voice actor for both of them, had to re-record the lines for Lemm because Team Cherry told him that Lemm is supposed to be annoyed with the Knight. Lemm's old lines were subsequently reused for the Nailsmith.
Lemm is the author behind the description for the  Void Idol.
The combined sell price of all Relics in the entire game is .
If you destroy any Dreamers before entering fountain square, the cutscene with Hornet is cancelled, due to Lemm being there.

---

---

# 文档：Little Fool

- 类别：characters
- 标识：little-fool
- 来源：wiki/Little_Fool

<b>Little Fool</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He collects the participation fees from contestants to the Colosseum of Fools.

Lore
Little Fool is a dedicated supporter of the Fools battling for glory in the Colosseum's Trials. He himself dreams of proving his strength in battle to Lord Fool.

In-game events
Little Fool is found chained up, hanging upside-down at the entrance of the Colosseum. Despite his predicament, he informs the Knight of the rules of the arena and takes payment to unlock each of the three Trials.
For , he unlocks the Trial of the Warrior
For , he unlocks the Trial of the Conqueror
For , he unlocks the Trial of the Fool

Location
Little Fool is located in the Colosseum of Fools, in the entrance room.

References

---

---

# 文档：Lurien

- 类别：characters
- 标识：lurien
- 来源：wiki/Lurien

<b>Lurien the Watcher</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He is one of the Dreamers and is involved in the Knight quest to destroy the Infection.

Lore
Lurien used to watch over the City of Tears from the Watcher's Spire. The Watcher Knights were his guards, and he had various servants such as his Cowardly Husk. At the top of his Spire, Lurien had a large telescope that looks down over the City of Tears, contributing to his title of "The Watcher".

After the Infection appeared, Lurien became part of the Pale King plan to seal the Radiance away in the Hollow Knight. He volunteered to be a Dreamer out of loyalty to the Pale King and to protect the City of Tears and the kingdom of Hallownest. Lurien and the other Dreamers sealed the entrance to the Black Egg in the Temple of the Black Egg, which put them into an eternal sleep. He and the other Dreamers attempt to lock the Knight in the Dream Realm after the Resting Grounds encounter.

Once the Knight reaches his physical body in the Watcher's Spire after defeating the Watcher Knights, his mind can be entered by using the Dream Nail. He can then be attacked in his Dream_Realm#Dreamers_Stage_1 after which Focus can be used to break his Seal. His physical body has disappeared when the Knight wakes up.

Location
Lurien's physical body is located in Watcher's Spire in the City of Tears. The Watcher Knights need to be defeated to reach him.

Achievements

Trivia
When Lurien has been killed, all the previously lit candles in his chamber are extinguished after exiting his dream.
There is a hidden room in the Watcher's Spire which contains Lurien's Lore_Tablets_(Hollow_Knight)#City_of_Tears. It can be accessed after taking the lift up to Lurien and breaking the third statue.
Early in development Lurien was known as "Lurian, The Whisperer".

Like the rest of the Dreamers, Lurien has numerous Cut Content (Hollow Knight) of dialogue which can still be found in the game's files.
When trying to attack Lurien's physical body in the Watcher's Spire, a Seals appears over his body.
Internally, Lurien has a suggestion of a relationship with another character in the game that is never hinted at in any of the visible lore. Team Cherry did not add this to the game because they did not want to reveal too much about some of the Dreamers.

---

---

# 文档：Marissa

- 类别：characters
- 标识：marissa
- 来源：wiki/Marissa

<b>Marissa</b> is a Spirits in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She was a songstress in the Pleasure House in the City of Tears.

Lore
Marissa was a songstress of some renown in Hallownest capital. She performed in the Pleasure House for enrapt crowds, with a voice that was described as being able to ease burdens and still troubled minds. When the Infection spread across Hallownest, Marissa's audiences eventually stopped coming to her performances.

In-game events

If the Knight has the Dream Nail, Marissa can be heard singing on her stage in the Pleasure House. She appears on the stage when the Knight approaches it. After explaining her backstory to the Knight, she asks if they would like to listen to her sing.

It is possible for the Knight to sit on the stage to listen to Marissa sing. Even though only the Knight is there to hear her, Marissa enjoys having an audience again.

Like all other spirits, the Dream Nail can be used on her, giving 1 Essence.

Location
Marissa is located in the Pleasure House in the City of Tears. A Simple Key is required to unlock the entrance to the Pleasure House.

Trivia

Marissa was first revealed in a Hollow Knight Kickstarter, where she was described as:
<i>Marissa the Butterfly is the ghost of a famous entertainer and musician in the Hallownest capital. The concept and dialogue for the character were submitted by Marissa Brice. Thanks Marissa!</i>
Marissa's singing is used in the beginning of the soundtrack version of the City of Tears music as well as in the music playing outside in the City of Tears.

---

---

# 文档：Mask Maker

- 类别：characters
- 标识：mask-maker
- 来源：wiki/Mask_Maker

<b>Mask Maker</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. They provide lore on masks and on Hallownest history.

Lore
Mask Maker provides "faces" for the faceless of the Kingdom. They compare a mask to a face and believe that a face is needed to define, to focus, and to exist.

While they say that they provide masks for the Kingdom, none of the masks which can be seen in their room correspond to the head of any bug found in Hallownest. Mask Maker comments on this, claiming that in Hallownest it is difficult to decipher whether a bug's face is a mask or their actual face.

Mask Maker knows about the Vessels corpses sealed in the Abyss and about the ancient caste's attempts at ruling the entirety of Hallownest. They also have knowledge of the Knight Void nature.

In-game events
Mask Maker is found in the northwest corner of Deepnest. They sit behind a desk surrounded by masks of all shapes and sizes, mumbling to themself and frantically working on simultaneously carving and painting more masks. Upon entering the room, Mask Maker has a chance of wearing one of three different masks.

Performing the Desolate Dive/Descending Dark Spells on them reveals a grey face with swirling eyes. However, Mask Maker mentions that this face may simply be "another mask", and if it is true, we will never see what the mask maker truly looks like.

Location
Mask Maker is found in the northwest part of Deepnest.

---

---

# 文档：Mato

- 类别：characters
- 标识：mato
- 来源：wiki/Mato

<b>Nailmaster Mato</b> is one of three Nailmasters in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He teaches the Cyclone Slash to whoever is willing to become his disciple. Mato also appears as a Bosses (Hollow Knight) in the  Brothers Oro & Mato dual fight.

Lore
Mato learnt the Nail Arts from the Sly along with his brothers Oro and Sheo. He was not the most skilled disciple of the three, his favourite move being the Cyclone Slash. Yet, he had a passion for teaching and hoped to perpetuate the Nailsage's knowledge to the next generation of Nailmasters. His devotion to the Nail Arts made him very close to his brother Sheo, the most talented.

When the three disciples became confirmed Nailmasters, Mato decided to never meet his old master again before mastering his teachings. He established a hut at the top of the world in the Howling Cliffs to continue honing his skills. He did not leave Oro in good terms though, and still awaits the promise his brother made to him.

In-game events
Mato can be found in his hut in the Howling Cliffs. He recognizes the Knight for the fighter they are and does not waste time in making them his disciple.

The Nailmaster is overwhelmed with emotion after passing the Cyclone Slash on to the Knight, considering them his child. After this, he welcomes them warmly every time they visit, inviting them to meditate together. Mato also comments on the Nail Arts the Knight has learnt. While he still resents his brother Oro, he is delighted to learn that Sheo did not forsake the Nail Arts. He is also impressed to learn of the Knight being recognized as a Nailmaster by the Nailsage himself.

Mato's mind is summoned to Godhome by the Godseeker. He appears when his brother Oro first falls in combat against the Knight. Despite their mutual resentment, his love for his brothers and the Nail Arts takes over. He then joins Oro in his fight against his opponent. After both are defeated, they bow respectfully to the Knight. The Brothers Oro & Mato are mainly fought at the peak of the Pantheon of the Master and later within the Pantheon of Hallownest.

Location
Nailmaster Mato is found in the Howling Cliffs.

Trivia
Mato's hut was first shown in a Hollow Knight Kickstarter update where the Nailmasters were revealed.
After learning the Cyclone Slash Nail Art, the Knight can return to Mato's house. He asks the Knight to meditate with him, and a sit prompt appears beside him, where the Knight can stay for as long as they wish.

---

---

# 文档：Midwife

- 类别：characters
- 标识：midwife
- 来源：wiki/Midwife

<b>Midwife</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She gives lore on Herrah and Deepnest.

Lore
Midwife is a servant to Spider Tribe, although few need her services as a midwife since Deepnest's brood has fallen to the Infection. While she is willing to provide information on Herrah and the Weavers, her appetite often gets the better of her, making her try to eat others.

In-game events
Midwife can be found in the southwestern part of Deepnest near the Distant Village. After listening to her, she attempts to eat the Knight by opening her mask, revealing a mouth filled with teeth.

She cannot be damaged, and hitting her causes her to hide until re-entering the area.

Midwife has new dialogue when the  Weaversong Charms is equipped.

Location
Midwife is found behind a breakable wall at the end of the corridor accessible from the southeast exit of the Distant Village.

Trivia
Midwife is one of the few NPCs who can be "hit" with the Nail without taking damage.
Midwife is one of the few NPCs who is able to detect when they are being Dream Nailed.
Midwife is the only NPC who can hurt the Knight, but is never fought as an actual enemy.

References

---

---

# 文档：Millibelle

- 类别：characters
- 标识：millibelle
- 来源：wiki/Millibelle

<b>Millibelle the Banker</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She provides banking services for a single fee.

Lore
Despite the lack of activity in Hallownest, Millibelle opened a bank in a recluse and safe area in Fog Canyon, next to the abandoned Queen's Station. Instead of a vault, she uses her shell to store Geo - a detail she tries to brush off.

However, behind her kind and polite facade, Millibelle is a thief whose purpose of using her shell to store Geo is so she can simply run off with it easily. If she gets enough Geo and is given the chance, she abandons her position with all the stored earnings.

In-game events
Millibelle can be found in her bank at the base of Fog Canyon. For a small starting fee of , she provides a service to keep the Knight Geo safe. Geo deposited in her bank is retained upon death and can be withdrawn at any time, up to a maximum of .

If she holds  or more and the Knight rests at a Bench (Hollow Knight) or uses the Stagways, visiting the bank again reveals that Millibelle has abandoned her stand. Hitting the empty stand three times reveals that it was a 2D cutout. The bank sign also falls to the ground on the way out.

After she disappears from Fog Canyon, <b>Millibelle the Thief</b> can be found at the Hot Spring in the Pleasure House in the City of Tears. Here she can be hit with the Nail and bounced around harmlessly, causing Geo to fall out of her. The amount of Geo that can be reclaimed is roughly the amount of Geo deposited times 1.5 for a total of up to . Geo stops falling out of her if there are at least 81 different pieces of Geo that have not been picked up.

Location
Millibelle's bank is found just west of Queen's Station in Fog Canyon but she runs away to the Pleasure House if she is visited in Fog Canyon after depositing  or more and resting on a Bench or using the Stagways.

Trivia
Fragile Greed/ Unbreakable Greed has no effect on the amount of Geo the Knight gets back from her shell.
The  the Knight deposits when opening the bank account is not returned.
At the Hot Spring in the Pleasure House, she is called <b>Millibelle the Thief</b>.
She can be bounced out of the screen through the room's exit but does not change rooms. Exiting and re-entering puts her back as usual. This decreases how much Geo she can drop.
When  Grimmchild is not in its first Phase, it treats Millibelle in her bank as an Enemies (Hollow Knight) and attempts to attack her, making her cower and not interact.
She also cowers if the Knight has the  Carefree Melody Charm equipped.
Having either Charm equipped also causes Weaversong and Glowing Womb to attack her, though these do not prevent her from interacting when Grimmchild is in its first Phase.
Dreamshield "tinks" off of the bank stand as if it was an enemy or damageable object.
Millibelle is one of the few NPCs who can be "hit" with the Nail without taking damage.

Notes

References

---

---

# 文档：Monomon

- 类别：characters
- 标识：monomon
- 来源：wiki/Monomon

<b>Monomon the Teacher</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She is one of the Dreamers and is involved in the Knight quest to destroy the Infection.

Lore
Monomon used to be a chief researcher and teacher of Hallownest. Her Teacher's Archives in Fog Canyon contain strange, jumbled Lore Tablets (Hollow Knight)#City of Tears that contain certain phrases such as:
"KINGLIGHT" and "OLDLIGHT", which are probably the Pale King and the Radiance.
"VESSEL" and "VOID", which are the Vessels and the Void.
"SEAL", "EGG", and "DREAMER", which seem to explain how the Radiance is sealed.
The archives also contain a tank full of Charged Lumafly, presumably there in order to study them. Monomon is hinted to have sourced the creatures resembling jellyfish found in Fog Canyon, if not only by accident.

When the Infection appeared, Monomon took part in the Pale King plan to seal the Radiance away in the Hollow Knight. Monomon became a Dreamer to seal the entrance to the Black Egg in the Temple of the Black Egg, which put her into an eternal sleep.

Quirrel was Monomon's apprentice or assistant. She entrusted him with her mask which serves as additional protection for her once she became a Dreamer. Monomon called Quirrel back to Hallownest to undo this protection after the Hollow Knight weakened. However, she and the other Dreamers still attempt to lock the Knight in the Dream Realm after the Resting Grounds encounter.

Once the Knight and Quirrel reach her physical body in the Teacher's Archives, her mind can be entered by using the Dream Nail. Monomon accepts that this must happen and seems to welcome it. She can then be attacked in her Dream Realm#Dreamers Stage 1 after which Focus can be used to break her Seal. Her physical body has disappeared when the Knight wakes up.

Location
Monomon's physical body can be found in the Teacher's Archives in Fog Canyon. Uumuu needs to be defeated to reach her.

Achievements

Trivia

A Cut Content (Hollow Knight) of the Elegy for Hallownest which has additional lines can still be found in the game files. Monomon herself also has numerous unused lines of dialogue.
When trying to attack Monomon's physical body in the Teacher's Archives, a Seals appears over her body.
In Team Cherry previous game, <i>Hungry Knight</i>, there are three masks found on the bosses that look similar to Monomon's.
In the original concepts for the Dreamers, all three Dreamers looked identical; sharing Monomon's mask and having the body shape of Lurien. The identical masks can still be seen on the archways outside the Temple of the Black Egg, and on one of the Hollow Knight promotional posters. The identical bodies can still be found on the Hollow Knight fountain in the City of Tears.

---

---

# 文档：Moss Prophet

- 类别：characters
- 标识：moss-prophet
- 来源：wiki/Moss_Prophet

<b>Moss Prophet</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. They provide lore on the Infection and the Radiance.

Lore
While most Mosskin Tribe follow Unn, Moss Prophet preaches about the Radiance's light. They believe that Hallownest will be reborn once it is united in her blazing image. Moss Prophet warns that suppressing the Radiance's light causes the Infection.

In-game events
Moss Prophet is found preaching about the Radiance to a group of Mossy Vagabonds in a small room in Queen's Gardens known as the "Moss Chapel". Moss Prophet and their followers are in the early stages of the Infection. When the Knight engages the Mossy Vagabonds that sit around them, Moss Prophet hides, but once they are dead, Moss Prophet resumes singing.

After acquiring the Monarch Wings or defeating one of the three Dreamers, Moss Prophet can be found dead along with the three Mossy Vagabonds, completely overtaken by the Infection.

Location
Moss Prophet's room can be entered from the Fungal Wastes, Deepnest, and the west part of the Queen's Gardens. The room's connection to the Queen's Gardens can only be opened from the Queen's Gardens itself.

Trivia
Moss Prophet is the only moss creature who does not attack the Knight. They are also the only moss creature with regular dialogue.

References

---

---

# 文档：Nymm

- 类别：characters
- 标识：nymm
- 来源：wiki/Nymm

<b>Nymm</b> is an NPCs (Hollow Knight) in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He gives the Carefree Melody Charms to the Knight.

Lore
Nymm is a bug who arrives in Dirtmouth after The Grimm Troupe (Lore) is banished. He plays an accordion-like bug to bring cheer to the fading town, although he admits that even his song is a little on the sadder side. Nymm is content to be in Dirtmouth beside the company of the Knight.

He greatly resembles Brumm without his mask. Nymm has no memory of why he travelled to Dirtmouth, or where he came from.

In-game events
If the Grimm Troupe is banished from Hallownest, Nymm appears in Dirtmouth beside the Bench (Hollow Knight). If the Ritual is completed by defeating Nightmare King Grimm, Nymm does not appear.

Nymm gives the Knight the Carefree Melody Charm when he is listened to.

Location
Nymm is located in Dirtmouth, next to the Bench.

Trivia
The music Nymm plays, Accordion Nymm, has the same melody as the Dirtmouth theme.

---

---

# 文档：Oro

- 类别：characters
- 标识：oro
- 来源：wiki/Oro

<b>Nailmaster Oro</b> is one of three Nailmasters in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He teaches the Dash Slash Nail Art to those willing to pay him for his services. Oro also appears as a boss as part of the  Brothers Oro & Mato dual fight.

Lore
Oro learnt the Nail Arts from the Sly along with his brothers Mato and Sheo. Of the three disciples, he was not the most skilled, with his signature move being the Dash Slash. While Oro is respectful of the Nailsage's teachings, he was reluctant to obey them to the letter.

Oro always seemed haunted by something. After a falling out among the Nailmasters, he chose to move away in seclusion. He did not leave his brother Mato in good terms and still owes him something. He sits in a hut in the far reaches of the Kingdom's Edge to avoid company.

In-game events
Oro can be found in his hut at the end of Kingdom's Edge. Reluctant of the Knight company, however, unlike the other Nailmasters he will charge you  to learn the Dash slash.

While always being uninviting, Oro does not hide his appreciation of the Knight's skills as a disciple. He comments on each of the Nail Arts the Knight has learnt while remembering his brothers and master. He even asks them to tell Mato to wait a bit longer before Oro gives him what he wants. When the Knight finally becomes a Nailmaster, Oro hopes that his participation in their training helped redeemed him a bit towards the Nailsage.

Oro's mind is summoned to Godhome by the Godseeker. There, he defies the Knight to prove his worth in the Nail Arts. Upon being seemingly defeated, his brother Mato appears. While they stare at first at each other with unveiled intensity, Mato's unexpected assistance invigorates Oro. They then help each other to fight the Knight. After both are defeated, they bow respectfully to their opponent. The Brothers Oro & Mato are mainly fought at the peak of the Pantheon of the Master and later within the Pantheon of Hallownest.

Additionally, the Knight can bring a Delicate Flower to Oro in his hut in Kingdom's Edge. He refuses it, telling the Knight he would throw it away if they left it to him. Yet, if given the Delicate Flower, Oro puts it in a vase upon the Knight's return to his hut.

Location
Nailmaster Oro is found in Kingdom's Edge.

Trivia

Oro is the only Nailmaster who must be paid in order to learn his Nail Art.
To the east of Oro's house is the impaled corpse of a Great Hopper dressed in a Nailmaster outfit, used as a training dummy. Hitting it with the Dream Nail reveals the dialogue: <i>"Stronger... Brothers..."</i>. When it is attacked enough times (25 attacks), two live Great Hoppers fall from the ceiling and attack the Knight.
Leaving the room resets the dummy, allowing it to be attacked again to summon two more Great Hoppers. This can make getting its Hunter's Journal entry easier.

---

---

# 文档：Salubra

- 类别：characters
- 标识：salubra
- 来源：wiki/Salubra

<b>Salubra</b> is a slug NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. She collects and sells Charms as well as Charm Notches.

Lore
Salubra's shop is located above the abandoned village at the bottom of the Forgotten Crossroads, near the Blue Lake. She is knowledgeable in the craft and use of Charms and eagerly shares them with others for Geo. She seems to be under the delusion that the village outside her house is still lively and is unaware of the Kingdom's demise. She is also very fond of the looks of the Knight.

In-game events
Accessing Salubra's shop requires defeating the Gruz Mother Bosses (Hollow Knight), and then at least acquiring the Mothwing Cloak or Mantis Claw, or Nail#Nail-bouncing on a Shade. Alternatively, she can be reached by crossing the Blue Lake in the Resting Grounds.

Salubra sells Charms and Notches but also shares tips on their use. After collecting all 40 Charms, she sells the Knight her Salubra's Blessing for , which regenerates SOUL while resting at a Bench (Hollow Knight). Upon buying her Blessing, a cutscene plays where she leans in and kisses the Knight.

scope="col" style="width: 96px;"| Item
scope="col" | Description
scope="col" style="width: 5rem;"| Cost
scope="col" style="min-width: 10rem;"| Requires

<i>Have you ever drunk that bright blue liquid, 'lifeblood'? It's a bit of a taboo, but it makes you feel much healthier doesn't it?

<i>That nail you wield is adorable! But do you sometimes feel it could be a little... longer?

<i>I love the simple design on this little fellow! Very classy! It's made out of a nice, solid, heavy material too.

<i>Are you a spellcaster, you little scoundrel? Ho ho! I'm only teasing.

<i>The crystal set in the centre is lovely, isn't it? Exquisite, even! I've heard that crystals help you to "focus", so if that's something you're interested in you should take this beauty home!</i>

<b>Before having enough Charms:</b>

Own 5 Charms

<b>Before having enough Charms:</b>

Own 10 Charms

<b>Before having enough Charms:</b>

Own 18 Charms

<b>Before having enough Charms:</b>

Own 25 Charms

<b>Before having enough Charms:</b>

Own 40 Charms

Location
Salubra's shop is located at the bottom of the Forgotten Crossroads. It can be accessed with the Mothwing Cloak or Mantis Claw, by Nail-bouncing on the Shade, or from the Blue Lake.

Achievements

Trivia
Salubra has Cut Content (Hollow Knight) where she recommends buying a Notch after a certain number of Charms have been obtained.
In the Beneath and Beyond trailer, Salubra can be heard with a different, higher pitched voice.
When obtaining Salubra's Blessing, a distorted version of the normal sound for getting Items and Abilities is played, similar to obtaining the Void Heart.
In the release version of the game, Salubra's Blessing only required 36 charms.  The Grimm Troupe increased the requirement to 40 as new Charms were added.
Having half of Kingsoul does not count as a Charm and does not count towards any of the purchases from Salubra.
When  Divine has a Charm consumed, the number of Charms needed for purchases from Salubra is reduced by one.
When  Grimmchild is lost by banishing the Grimm Troupe, the number of Charms needed for purchases from Salubra is reduced by one. Obtaining Carefree Melody returns this lost Charm.

References

---

# 文档：Sheo

- 类别：characters
- 标识：sheo
- 来源：wiki/Sheo

<b>Nailmaster Sheo</b> is one of three Nailmasters in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. Although retired, he teaches the Great Slash Nail Art to those seeking him. Sheo also appears as a boss as  Paintmaster Sheo.

Lore
Sheo learnt the Nail Arts from the Sly, along with his brothers Oro and Mato. Despite his aloof and kind demeanour, he was the most skilled disciple, driven solely by his desire for strength and the mastering of his skills. Yet, this passion faded over time, as he desired to explore new forms of arts that could move him. He still had a good relationship with both of his quarrelling brothers and had respect for his master.

After some time, Sheo hung up his nail to become an artist. He set a hut in a far corner of Greenpath where he experiments various forms of creative art in peace. He hopes to be able to show how far he has come in his craft to his old teacher.

In-game events

Sheo can be found in his hut in Greenpath. When the Knight comes by, the retired Nailmaster is painting an image of a bug shell. Facing the Knight's disinterest in his arts, Sheo finally concedes in teaching them his signature move, the Great Slash. He, however, chooses to do so using only his brush, explaining that it is the same as a nail to him.

After his lesson, Sheo invites the Knight to stay and paint with him whenever they visit. He recognizes when the Knight has learnt a new Nail Art from his brothers, and hopes that it brought happiness to them both. He also takes notices of the Nailsage's acknowledgement of the Knight as Nailmaster. This moves him, but not enough to make him stray from his current path of artist.

If the Nailsmith was spared after forging the Nail#Pure Nail, he eventually finds his way to Sheo's hut. Seeing the Nailsmith distraught about his own life, they talk and discover their common love of creation and honing a skill. From this point, Sheo starts teaching the Nailsmith whichever art he experimented in so far. They eventually move on to crafting models of the Five Great Knights of Hallownest. Sheo remembers them well, while mentioning Hegemol was his favourite. The Achievements (Hollow Knight) name, <i>Happy Couple</i>, implies Sheo and the Nailsmith are in a romantic relationship.

Sheo's mind is summoned to Godhome by The Godseeker ritual. However he appears there not as the Nailmaster he was, but as the Paintmaster he became. As such, he battles the Knight with his brush and paints, expressing formidable strength in doing so, and bows to them once defeated. While in Godhome, Sheo also takes inspiration from his host's motivation and quest for his own pursuit of arts. Paintmaster Sheo is mainly fought at the peak of the Pantheon of the Artist and later within the Pantheon of Hallownest.

Location
Nailmaster Sheo is found in Greenpath, at the end of a spike-filled tunnel. Either Crystal Heart or Monarch Wings are required to reach him.

Achievements

---

---

# 文档：Sly

- 类别：characters
- 标识：sly
- 来源：wiki/Sly

<b>Sly</b> is a NPCs (Hollow Knight)#Merchants in <i>Hollow Knight (game)</i>. He sells many wares at his shop in Dirtmouth for Geo but drives a hard bargain. Sly also appears as a boss as the  Great Nailsage Sly.

Lore
Sly used to be the Great Nailsage. During his previous occupation, he trained some disciples in the Nail Arts: Oro, Mato, and Sheo.

Eventually, Sly abandoned his occupation as Nailsage. He became convinced of the strength of accumulating Geo over honing one's nail. He thus turned to the simple life of a merchant, finding and selling wares from his shop in Dirtmouth. He did not, however, forget his mastery and respect of the nail and those who practice its arts.  He keeps his greatnail in the basement of his shop and still remembers his pupils.

At some point, a dream led him to wander off into the caverns below Dirtmouth. He also lost his Shopkeeper's Key which ended up in a room in Crystal Peak.

In-game events
Sly can be found in a hovel in the abandoned village at the bottom of the Forgotten Crossroads, past the Gruz Mother. The shopkeeper has been slowly succumbing to the Infection, but approaching him breaks him out of his daze, saving him. After which, Sly returns to Dirtmouth to open his shop, without any recollection of how he fell into that predicament.

Back in Dirtmouth, Sly sells various wares: from Charms to adventuring gear, even Mask Shards and Vessel Fragments. His backstock, however, remains locked until he is returned his Shopkeeper's Key, lost in the Crystal Peak.

Sly notes when the Knight has honed their Nail, and also when they start learning Nail Arts. After they learn all three Nail Arts from the Nailmasters, Sly can be found in his basement, where he reveals himself as the legendary Nailsage. To honour the rites of passage of Nailmasters, he gifts the Knight the Nailmaster's Glory Charm.

As part of The Godseeker ritual, Sly's mind is summoned to Godhome. There he takes his original mantle of Great Nailsage Sly and fights the Knight, his thoughts focused solely on his days past. Upon defeat, Sly bows respectfully to the Knight. He is fought at the peak of the Pantheon of the Sage and later within the Pantheon of Hallownest.

style="width: 8rem;" | Item
Description
style="min-width: 5rem;" | Cost
style="min-width: 12rem;" | Requires

<i>Simply, a simple key. It will fit a variety of locks, which is useful if you like to poke around in places you don't belong.</i>

No conditions.

<i>I found this under the counter. Some creature must have laid it here while I was stuck down in the ruins.

No conditions.

<i>What's more important? A light to guide your way, or a friend who'll stay by your side?

No conditions.

<i>Do you find yourself leaving a lot of Geo behind as you hurry through the caverns?

No conditions.

<i>Life in Hallownest can be tough, always taking hits and getting knocked around. This charm grants you more time to recover after taking damage. Useful if you need to escape from a tight spot.</i>

No conditions.

<i>You enjoy smacking about foes with that nail of yours, right? With this charm equipped, you'll be able to send them flying further with every hit!</i>

Requires Shopkeeper's Key

<i>An explorer found this fancy key floating in the waterways of the city far below us. I've not cleaned it.</i>

Requires Shopkeeper's Key

<i>This old thing? Merely a fragment of something larger. Still, maybe you can make some use of it.</i>

No conditions.

<i>I found another of these. If you look closely, there's some nice engraving work on them.

Requires purchasing previous Mask Shard

<i>You're collecting these, aren't you? Well you're in luck, I found another in my storeroom.

Requires Shopkeeper's Key and purchasing previous Mask Shard

<i>As it turns out, I do actually have one more of these shards left. Very last one!

Requires Shopkeeper's Key and purchasing previous Mask Shard

<i>A piece of a Soul Vessel. You know, those ghastly looking orb things?

No conditions.

<i>I dug out another of these. Turns out I had a complete set. If they didn't give me the creeps, I'd put the pieces together myself.</i>

Requires Shopkeeper's Key and purchasing previous Vessel Fragment

<i>You like it? It's a recent addition to my collection. Handsome thing, isn't it?

Requires Shopkeeper's Key

Location
Sly first appears in an abandoned house in the southeast region of the Forgotten Crossroads. After listening to him, he can next be found in Dirtmouth.

Trivia
Sly being more than he seems is repeatedly foreshadowed. For instance, his very first line, "Oro, you oaf... you wield your nail... like a club..." refers to Nailmaster Oro, and he comments on the Knight’s Nail several times.
According to a livestream Q&A, the development team originally planned for Sly to be a difficult boss who wielded a nail approximately the size of the other Nailmasters'. This idea was later reused for the  <i>Godmaster</i> content pack.
Sly mentions a character named "Esmy" in one of his lines of dialogue. This character is never seen in the game nor mentioned again. In a Reddit AMA it is stated that Esmy is someone that still lingers in Sly's dreams.

As the  Great Nailsage, Sly's cape is a pinkish colour as opposed to blue.
Sly mentions owning a full set of Vessel Fragments, although he only sells two out of three; this is possibly attributed to Soul Vessels being comprised of only two Vessel Fragments at one point before being changed by release.
The Ferocious Foes trailer also shows Sly selling half of a Soul Vessel.
Although removed from the game, two Cut Content (Hollow Knight) can be seen on a shelf in Sly's shop.
Sly had a different voice actor in the <i>Hollow Knight Beta</i>.

---

---

# 文档：Spirits

- 类别：characters
- 标识：spirits
- 来源：wiki/Spirits

<b>Spirits</b> are the remains of a deceased bug given visible form. They are only visible in-game after acquiring the Dream Nail. All Spirits, except for the Traitors' Child, can be consumed by hitting them with the Dream Nail to acquire 1 Essence; however, this does not count towards completion and the Essence is not necessary to fully awaken the Dream Nail.

These Spirits are dream-made entities who take the shape of a bug who has passed away. As such, most of them seem to be under the assumption that they are living as the bug in question. They are all encountered floating above or near the real bug's deceased body, thus many of them can be encountered in graveyards, such as the Resting Grounds. Almost all of them do not move from their spot, and offer dialogue when interacted with.
